meta.resolution_methodology: KB-driven query-based resolution with anti-hallucination verification

test_cases.title: Verify User Registration with Valid Data
test_cases.resolution_summary: This test case requires a user registration page with username, email, password, confirm password, first name, last name, phone number, country dropdown, and register button. The AURA framework does NOT have a dedicated RegistrationPage page object. The framework uses HPIDPage for HP ID account creation, which handles email, password, firstName, and lastName fields, but does NOT have username, confirm_password, phone_number, or country fields. The test requires creating a new RegistrationPage page object with all required locators and methods, or adapting the test to use the existing HPIDPage if the application under test uses HP ID authentication.
test_cases.kb_verification_notes: KB confirmed that HPIDPage exists for account creation but does not contain the specific fields required by this test case (username, confirm_password, phone_number, country). KB confirmed no other registration page object exists. KB confirmed foundation files (test_flows_common, playwright_manager, base_page_object, conftest) exist and must be included.

relevant_locators_summary.NOTE: HPIDPage does NOT contain username_input, email_input, password_input, confirm_password_input, first_name_input, last_name_input, phone_number_input, country_dropdown, or register_button locators required by this test case. These must be created.

test_cases.flow_pattern: Registration Form Fill → Submit → Success Page Verification

components_to_create.description: Complete page object for user registration form with all required fields: username, email, password, confirm_password, first_name, last_name, phone_number, country dropdown, and register button. Must inherit from BasePageObject.
components_to_create.reason: KB confirmed no existing page object has these fields. HPIDPage only has email, password, firstName, lastName but lacks username, confirm_password, phone_number, and country fields.

critical_gaps.gap: No RegistrationPage page object exists in the framework
critical_gaps.impact: Cannot automate this test case without creating a complete new page object with 10+ locators and 10+ methods
critical_gaps.recommendation: Create pages/registration_page.py with all required locators and methods, OR adapt the test case to use HPIDPage if the application uses HP ID authentication

critical_gaps.gap: No SuccessPage page object exists for generic registration confirmation
critical_gaps.impact: Cannot verify registration success without creating success page object
critical_gaps.recommendation: Create pages/success_page.py with success_message locator and verification methods, OR use ThankYouPage if it's applicable to this registration flow

critical_gaps.gap: HPIDPage lacks username, confirm_password, phone_number, and country fields
critical_gaps.impact: Cannot use existing HPID authentication flow for this test case
critical_gaps.recommendation: If the application under test uses HP ID authentication, coordinate with development team to understand if these fields exist in the actual application or if the test case needs to be updated

alternative_approaches.description: If the application under test uses HP ID authentication (which only requires email, password, firstName, lastName), adapt the test case to remove username, confirm_password, phone_number, and country fields and use existing HPIDHelper.sign_up() method
alternative_approaches.recommendation: Recommended if application uses HP ID authentication

alternative_approaches.description: Create a new pages/registration_page.py with all required locators and methods for the generic registration form described in the test case
alternative_approaches.recommendation: Recommended if application has a custom registration form different from HP ID

alternative_approaches.description: If the application has a user creation API, use API calls to create the user account and only test the UI verification steps
alternative_approaches.recommendation: Consider if API exists and test focus is on verification rather than form interaction

summary.framework_coverage_assessment: The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment flows using HP ID authentication. This test case requires a generic user registration form with fields (username, confirm_password, phone_number, country) that do not exist in the HP ID authentication flow. The framework has strong foundation files and infrastructure but lacks the specific page objects needed for this test case.
recommendations: Clarify if the application under test uses HP ID authentication or a custom registration form
If HP ID: Adapt test case to use existing HPIDPage and HPIDHelper (remove username, confirm_password, phone_number, country fields)
If custom registration: Create pages/registration_page.py and pages/success_page.py with all required components
Consider creating a registration helper (helper/registration_helper.py) to orchestrate the multi-step registration flow
Ensure test data includes unique email generation to avoid duplicate user conflicts
Add API-based cleanup to delete test users after execution
